title: IOM / FEX to Fabric Interconnect Link Flap Pairs (UCSM Syslog)
exclusions: Fabric Interconnect *Northbound* Ethernet port-channels to the data-centre LAN or Nexus vPC (MO family `fabric/lan/‚Ä¶/pc-‚Ä¶` and LACP member health) ‚Äî that is UC-19.1.5 and upstream switch work, not a chassis/IFM/FEX to FI hop. vNIC- or vSwitch-side misconfiguration and host OS driver flaps (no `sys/‚Ä¶/fabric` or FEX `host/port` in the `dn`). FCoE-only or FC ports that do not produce these Ethernet `UCSM-*-LINK_*` lines in your build. Intentional UCSM *maintenance* events that legitimately restart every IOM uplink in the same two-minute window (firmware) ‚Äî use `ucs_change_windows` suppression or freeze alerts. Intersight-only or IMM-only domains with no `cisco:ucs:syslog` in Splunk. Hard-down links with no `UCSM-6-LINK_UP` within 300s ‚Äî not counted as a flap *pair* here; treat as sustained outage with 19.1.5.
dataSources: `index=cisco_ucs` with sourcetype `cisco:ucs:syslog` from UCS Manager via syslog (Fabric Interconnect or UCSM service plane). The message line follows Cisco UCSM‚Äôs `%UCSM-*` token, bracketed F-code, and MO `dn` in `[‚Ä¶]`. Complementary: `cisco:ucs:fault` for persistent `faultInst` rows (F0408, F0282) polled via the TA XML path ‚Äî used for triage, not the primary rolling flap counter.
app: Splunk Connect for Syslog (SC4S) *or* Universal Forwarder `[udp://]` / `[tcp-ssl:]` to receive syslog; `Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs` ([Splunkbase 2731](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2731)) for orgs that colocate the TA‚Äôs parsing with UCS inputs. HEC to index `cisco_ucs`.
spl:
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:syslog" earliest=-24h
# Anchor on real UCSM tokens (UCSM-3-LINK_DOWN / UCSM-6-LINK_UP) and the bracketed MO path. Do not use a bare "link down" keyword search.
| rex field=_raw "UCSM-(?P<sevnum>[0-9]+)-(?P<msgid>[A-Za-z0-9_-]+):"
| eval message_code=if(isnotnull(msgid) AND isnotnull(sevnum), "UCSM-".sevnum."-".msgid, null())
| rex field=_raw "\[(?P<dn>sys/[^\]]+)\]"
| eval dn=replace(dn, "/state$", "")
| where isnotnull(dn) AND (match(dn, "(?i)chassis-[^/]+/slot-[^/]+/fabric/port-") OR match(dn, "(?i)chassis-[^/]+/slot-[^/]+/ifm-") OR match(dn, "(?i)fex-[^/]+/slot-[^/]+/host/port-") OR match(dn, "(?i)switch-(A|B)/slot-[^/]+/switch-ether/port-"))
| where message_code IN ("UCSM-3-LINK_DOWN", "UCSM-6-LINK_UP")
| sort 0 _time
| transaction dn maxspan=300s maxevents=10 startswith=eval(message_code="UCSM-3-LINK_DOWN") endswith=eval(message_code="UCSM-6-LINK_UP")
| where closed_txn=1
| stats count as flap_pairs by dn
| rex field=dn "chassis-(?P<chassis_id>[0-9]+)"
| rex field=dn "fex-(?P<fex_id>[0-9]+)"
| rex field=dn "slot-(?P<slot>[0-9]+)"
| rex field=dn "port-(?P<port>[0-9]+)"
| rex field=dn "switch-(?P<fi_id>[AB])"
| eval fi_id=coalesce(fi_id, "unspecified")
| eval severity_bucket=case(flap_pairs>=10, "critical", flap_pairs>=5, "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval Alert_severity=case(severity_bucket=="critical", "high", severity_bucket=="warning", "medium", 1=1, "low")
| lookup ucs_chassis_slot_optic_inventory chassis_id slot port OUTPUT optic_pn optic_serial install_date replacement_history
| eval last_replacement_date=coalesce(replacement_history, install_date)
| lookup ucs_fabric_port_owners fi_id port OUTPUT primary_team
| eval recommended_action=case(severity_bucket=="critical", "Page on-call: replace optic or DAC; cross-check cisco:ucs:fault for F0408/F0282; correlate UC-19.1.13 CRCs", severity_bucket=="warning", "Dispatch: reseat, clean end-faces, schedule RMA from replacement_history", 1=1, "Log under-threshold flaps to dashboard only")
| eval runbook_url="https://wiki.example.com/runbook/uc-19-1-35-iom-fex-fi-link-flap"
| table chassis_id, fex_id, slot, port, fi_id, dn, flap_pairs, severity_bucket, Alert_severity, optic_pn, optic_serial, last_replacement_date, recommended_action, runbook_url, primary_team
| sort 0 - flap_pairs, severity_bucket
description: Detects *completed* IOM, IFM, FEX-host, or FI switch-ether `dn` link micro-flap *pairs* (UCSM `UCSM-3-LINK_DOWN` followed by `UCSM-6-LINK_UP` within 300s on the same `dn`) in `cisco:ucs:syslog`, tallies them over 24h, and tiers severity (5+=warning, 10+=critical) with optic and owner lookups ‚Äî without treating bare English ‚Äúlink down‚Äù as a search keyword.
value: Flapping trunks between chassis I/O, rack FEX, and the Fabric Interconnects create intermittent brown-outs: storage paths time out, live migrations wobble, and the same SFP can test clean in a five-minute window yet fail under thermal load. Pairing *down* and *up* on the same MO path removes false ‚Äúflap‚Äù counts from hard-downs and from unrelated text, while the 24h histogram gives you a defensible, hardware-ticket narrative before the Northbound LAG in UC-19.1.5 ever turns red. The business outcome is fewer unexplained P3s, faster optic RMA, and a chain of evidence (serial, team, `dn`, timestamp) the network and compute owners can share without finger-pointing meetings.
implementation: Enable UCSM syslog to SC4S or a UF, assign `cisco:ucs:syslog` and `cisco_ucs` index, deploy the `props` extractions, load the two CSV lookups, run the `spl` on a 30m schedule with 4h per-`dn` throttle, and wire PagerDuty/ServiceNow by `severity_bucket`.
visualization: Bar chart: `flap_pairs` by `dn` (24h). Table: `dn`, `flap_pairs`, `severity_bucket`, `optic_serial`, `primary_team`. Timechart: count of `closed_txn` flaps from a companion one-line search. Single value: critical count. **Pair with UC-19.1.13** in the same row for CRC+flap triangulation.
dataModelAcceleration: No requirement for CIM on raw `cisco:ucs:syslog` for this use case. If you normalise to an intermediate summary index, accelerate that summary, not a fictional CIM data model for UCS syslog.

references.title: Cisco UCS Manager System Messages Reference (UCSM-* LINK / FSM messages)
Splunk CIM ‚Äî Network Traffic and Alerts data models
Cisco ‚Äî Integrate data with Splunk: Cisco UCS

knownFalsePositives: A coordinated UCSM / IOM / FEX *firmware* activation that reboots every server-facing link in a governed window (expect many `LINK_DOWN` without operational meaning once the change id is in place ‚Äî suppress with a time- and `dn`-prefix lookup). *Planned* cable re-seat or SFP RMA in a TAC-observed session that intentionally drops a single port. Lab `shutdown` / `no shutdown` on a test uplink to prove optics. *Port-channel* member add/remove on the **upstream** Nexus that briefly withdraws a LACP child toward the FI while the UCS MO still shows transition language ‚Äî that is a netops change story, not always a bad SFP, so reconcile the Cisco change ticket before paging. *Micro-flap storms* after data-centre *generator tests* that dip HVAC and temperature at the patch panel. *Duplicate syslog* lines at a site relay can double `flap_pairs` ‚Äî add `dedup` on `_raw` for that forwarder class only if your pipeline team proves duplication.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ `Splunk_TA_cisco_ucs` (Splunkbase 2731) on Search Heads for field discovery, **or** a dedicated parsing app you own with the `props`/`transforms` below on indexers. Heavy Forwarder optional unless you also poll XML.
‚Ä¢ A reachable HEC/SC4S or UF syslog path from **both** Fabric Interconnects; index role `cisco_ucs` for operators.
‚Ä¢ `lookups/ucs_chassis_slot_optic_inventory.csv` and `lookups/ucs_fabric_port_owners.csv` deployed to a custom app (not `search`) with `transforms.conf` and **Permissions ‚Üí** global read for alert runner roles.
‚Ä¢ UCSM 4.1+ / 4.2+ with syslog policy that includes informational **LINK_UP** lines.



**Step 1 ‚Äî Identify the problem and the signal (scope and risk)**

Why micro-flaps on the IOM, FEX, X-series IFM, or switch-ether path toward the Fabric Interconnect matter: each true physical-layer bounce is typically 200 to 800 milliseconds of forwarding discontinuity on that Ethernet segment. A single blip on a vNIC carrying NFS, iSCSI, or vSphere traffic is enough to elicit a TCP retransmit storm, a SCSI timeout retried by the array, a vMotion with a ‚Äúnetwork error‚Äù in the vCenter task, or a vSphere HA isolation response if heartbeats and storage paths align badly with the same window. Cumulative over time, a micro-flap does not have to open a P1 the way a long Northbound LAG down does (covered in UC-19.1.5) ‚Äî the damage is in repeated tail latency, brown-out behaviour, and help-desk ‚Äúintermittent network‚Äù language that is hard to reproduce if you only look at a five-minute average utilisation (UC-19.1.16) or a one-minute CRC delta (UC-19.1.13). The authoritative operational surface for the short narrative you need is the UCSM syslog line that Cisco emits when the I/O module or FEX port toward the fabric changes link state, because the Distinguished Name in the line tells you the exact managed object, not a hand-wavy ‚ÄúFEX-3‚Äù in an email. Contrast the hardware paths: B-series IOM fabric uplinks under `sys/chassis-N/slot-M/fabric/port-‚Ä¶` (sometimes extended with a `/state` tail in the text), C-series or rack FEX host ports under `sys/fex-N/slot-M/host/port-‚Ä¶`, and X-series IFM paths that include the `ifm` segment under the chassis, whereas Fabric Interconnect fixed-module Ethernet toward servers lives under `sys/switch-A|B/slot-‚Ä¶/switch-ether/port-‚Ä¶`. The Fabric Interconnect *Northbound* port-channel to the data-centre switch is a different `dn` family (`fabric/lan/‚Ä¶/pc-‚Ä¶`) and is *not* this use case. Likewise, a hard-down for more than five minutes without a `UCSM-6-LINK_UP` in the 300s transaction window is not scored here as a ‚Äúflap pair‚Äù; treat sustained loss with UC-19.1.5 and your cable matrix to the spine. Compliance mapping: NIST 800-53 **SI-4 (Information System Monitoring)** expects you to capture security- and safety-relevant operational anomalies on the management and data links you already agreed to log; the flap histogram you build in Splunk is a concrete, auditable artefact that ties back to a named MO path and a serialised optic record in a lookup, not a screenshot-only war story. Hardware reality on the wire: the UCS platform families that produce these paths include IOMs **2204XP**, **2208XP**, **2304**, **2408**; Fabric Interconnects **6248UP**, **6296UP**, **6332**, **6332-16UP**, **6454**, and **6536**; and FEXes such as **2232PP** and **2348UPQ**; X-series IFM SKUs like **UCSX-I-9108-25G** and **UCSX-I-9108-100G** carry the same logical risk at higher lane rates. Cabling: Twinax direct-attach, multimode and singlemode transceivers, and active optical cables (AOC) are all in play ‚Äî any one marginal connector can produce the syslog profile this UC counts.

**Step 2 ‚Äî Implement data collection, parsing, and enrichment**

**(A) Enable UCSM syslog in the fabric manager.** Set the syslog policy so **UCSM-6-LINK_UP** (informational) is not dropped while you still receive **UCSM-3-LINK_DOWN** (error-class). A CLI pattern teams use in lab is to scope **monitoring ‚Üí syslog**, enable forwarding, and set the printed severity to **informational**; commit, then verify both FIs in the pair point at the same receiver list. The exact `scope` chain must match the Cisco **Network Management** and **System Logging** section for your UCSM 4.1+ / 4.2+ build.

**(B) SC4S (Splunk Connect for Syslog) ‚Äî production-scale receiver.** Use the product‚Äôs **Cisco** / **UCSM**-aware filter so the vendor strip sets `sourcetype` to `cisco:ucs:syslog` and lands in your agreed index. Bind `splunk_metadata.csv` (or the YAML your SC4S release uses) to `index=cisco_ucs` and `sourcetype=cisco:ucs:syslog` on the HEC path. Expose **UDP/514** or **TCP-TLS/6514** and restrict the source to the two Fabric Interconnect VRF exports that send syslog, plus any SC4S cluster peers. After deploy, on the **SC4S** host run the documented filter/health check (your release will ship a `sc4s` or `syslogng` self-test) to prove the UCSM template is in the path before you accept production traffic. If you use a **Universal Forwarder** to collect syslog, SSH to the UF and run the shell (not SPL): `splunk btool inputs list cisco_ucs --debug` ‚Äî the prefix `cisco_ucs` is an example; replace with the app that owns your `udp://` stanza, or use `splunk btool inputs list --debug` and grep for your index name.

```ini
# app/p_local_sc4s/README or vendor doc ‚Äî do not copy vendor IDs blindly; the stanza *shape* is what matters
# HEC to Splunk: use the token the Security team issues; index and sourcetype are metadata on the HEC post
# Example Universal Forwarder on SC4S node reading local spool (site-specific; often unnecessary when SC4S posts HEC directly)
[monitor:///var/lib/syslog-splunk/cisco/ucs/ucsm-*.log]
sourcetype = cisco:ucs:syslog
index = cisco_ucs
disabled = 0
```

If your documentation references `Splunk_TA_cisco-ucs` as the syslog on-ramp, check **Settings ‚Üí Data inputs** for a **UDP/TCP** stanza (legacy) or a scripted forwarder; many green-field designs skip that and go straight to SC4S+HEC.

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_cisco_ucs_syslog/local/inputs.conf
[udp://:514]
connection_host = ip
sourcetype = cisco:ucs:syslog
index = cisco_ucs
```

**props.conf (Heavy Forwarder or Indexer tier where the sourcetype is first assigned):**

```ini
[cisco:ucs:syslog]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
TIME_FORMAT = %b %d %H:%M:%S
TIME_PREFIX = ^<\d+>
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 32
TZ = UTC
EXTRACT-ucsm_msg = ^.*?:\s+%(?<message_code>UCSM-\d+-\w+):\s+\[(?<fault_code>F[0-9]+)\]\[(?<sevword>\w+)\]\[(?<cause>[\w-]+)\]\[(?<dn>[^\]]+)\]\s*(?<message>.+)$
EXTRACT-dn_path = (?:sys/(?:chassis-(?<chassis_id>[0-9]+)|fex-(?<fex_id>[0-9]+)|switch-(?<fi_id>[AB]))(?:/slot-(?<slot>[0-9]+))?(?:(?:/fabric|/host|/ifm-[^/]+/[^/]+)/port-(?<port>[0-9]+))?)
```

**transforms.conf (optional sourcetype routing on a multi-vendor feed):**

```ini
[ucs_syslog_sourcetype]
REGEX = UCSM
DEST_KEY = MetaData:Sourcetype
FORMAT = cisco:ucs:syslog
```

**Lookup file `lookups/ucs_chassis_slot_optic_inventory.csv` (eight example rows, UTF-8, no BOM):**

```csv
chassis_id,slot,port,optic_pn,optic_serial,install_date,replacement_history
2,1,3,DS-SFP-FC8G-SW,SN12340001,2019-08-10,
2,1,4,SFP-10G-SR,SN9K550012,2021-11-15,2024-01-20 RMA serial SN9K550199
3,2,1,SFP-25G-SR-S,SN7G440011,2022-04-30,
4,1,2,QSFTE-40G,SN3C990022,2018-02-11,2019-09-12 TAC 692233 cable swap
1,1,1,SFP-H10GB-CU3M,SN-DAC-0101,2023-01-10,
5,1,4,SFP-25G-SR-S,SN7G440077,2023-10-15,
1,1,2,SFP-10G-SR,SN9K550333,2017-07-20,2020-10-10 aged optic
8,1,1,AOC-10G-3M,SN-AOC-0088,2024-03-22,
```

**`lookups/ucs_fabric_port_owners.csv` (four example rows):**

```csv
fi_id,port,primary_team
A,1,netcompute-fi-a@example.com
A,2,netcompute-fi-a@example.com
B,1,netcompute-fi-b@example.com
B,2,netcompute-fi-b@example.com
```

**transforms for lookups:**

```ini
[ucs_chassis_slot_optic_inventory]
filename = ucs_chassis_slot_optic_inventory.csv
[ucs_fabric_port_owners]
filename = ucs_fabric_port_owners.csv
```

**Optional** scripted XML API for `faultInst` to index `cisco:ucs:fault` (different sourcetype): a minimal request body to validate in a lab (session/cookie is site-specific) is a `configResolveClass` with `classId="faultInst"`; compare F0408 persistence against the flap pairs from syslog.

**SPL to save as report/alert (must match the `spl` field exactly):**

```spl
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:syslog" earliest=-24h
# Anchor on real UCSM tokens (UCSM-3-LINK_DOWN / UCSM-6-LINK_UP) and the bracketed MO path. Do not use a bare "link down" keyword search.
| rex field=_raw "UCSM-(?P<sevnum>[0-9]+)-(?P<msgid>[A-Za-z0-9_-]+):"
| eval message_code=if(isnotnull(msgid) AND isnotnull(sevnum), "UCSM-".sevnum."-".msgid, null())
| rex field=_raw "\[(?P<dn>sys/[^\]]+)\]"
| eval dn=replace(dn, "/state$", "")
| where isnotnull(dn) AND (match(dn, "(?i)chassis-[^/]+/slot-[^/]+/fabric/port-") OR match(dn, "(?i)chassis-[^/]+/slot-[^/]+/ifm-") OR match(dn, "(?i)fex-[^/]+/slot-[^/]+/host/port-") OR match(dn, "(?i)switch-(A|B)/slot-[^/]+/switch-ether/port-"))
| where message_code IN ("UCSM-3-LINK_DOWN", "UCSM-6-LINK_UP")
| sort 0 _time
| transaction dn maxspan=300s maxevents=10 startswith=eval(message_code="UCSM-3-LINK_DOWN") endswith=eval(message_code="UCSM-6-LINK_UP")
| where closed_txn=1
| stats count as flap_pairs by dn
| rex field=dn "chassis-(?P<chassis_id>[0-9]+)"
| rex field=dn "fex-(?P<fex_id>[0-9]+)"
| rex field=dn "slot-(?P<slot>[0-9]+)"
| rex field=dn "port-(?P<port>[0-9]+)"
| rex field=dn "switch-(?P<fi_id>[AB])"
| eval fi_id=coalesce(fi_id, "unspecified")
| eval severity_bucket=case(flap_pairs>=10, "critical", flap_pairs>=5, "warning", 1=1, "info")
| eval Alert_severity=case(severity_bucket=="critical", "high", severity_bucket=="warning", "medium", 1=1, "low")
| lookup ucs_chassis_slot_optic_inventory chassis_id slot port OUTPUT optic_pn optic_serial install_date replacement_history
| eval last_replacement_date=coalesce(replacement_history, install_date)
| lookup ucs_fabric_port_owners fi_id port OUTPUT primary_team
| eval recommended_action=case(severity_bucket=="critical", "Page on-call: replace optic or DAC; cross-check cisco:ucs:fault for F0408/F0282; correlate UC-19.1.13 CRCs", severity_bucket=="warning", "Dispatch: reseat, clean end-faces, schedule RMA from replacement_history", 1=1, "Log under-threshold flaps to dashboard only")
| eval runbook_url="https://wiki.example.com/runbook/uc-19-1-35-iom-fex-fi-link-flap"
| table chassis_id, fex_id, slot, port, fi_id, dn, flap_pairs, severity_bucket, Alert_severity, optic_pn, optic_serial, last_replacement_date, recommended_action, runbook_url, primary_team
| sort 0 - flap_pairs, severity_bucket
```

Walk-through: the `rex` lines derive `message_code` and `dn` if `props` did not already run, so a Heavy Forwarder without the TA can still self-contain. The `match` clauses scope to the chassis, IFM, FEX, and switch-ether DNs. The `transaction` enforces a completed down‚Üíup pair within 300s on the *same* `dn`; the `where closed_txn=1` gate drops incomplete stories (hard-down goes to UC-19.1.5). The `case()` applies default numeric thresholds; move those to a `csv` in Step 5 if you need per-chassis SLO. Lookups attach CMDB and paging. **CIM note:** `Alert_severity` is a string bucket that maps to **Alerts**-style `severity` if you add `FIELDALIAS-severity = Alert_severity AS severity` in `local/props` for a downstream CIM add-on. **NIST:** retain raw syslog for 90 days where policy allows; align retention to SI-4 evidence expectations.

**Step 3 ‚Äî Validate in Search, on the FIs, and in UCSM**

Confirm syslog is landing: `index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:syslog" earliest=-1h | head 5` and inspect `_raw` for lines shaped like the Cisco sample: `%UCSM-3-LINK_DOWN: [F0408]‚Ä¶[sys/chassis-‚Ä¶/fabric/port-‚Ä¶/state] ‚Ä¶`. If `head` returns nothing, the problem is HEC, SC4S, or firewall to the collector ‚Äî not the SPL. Field extraction: `‚Ä¶ | stats count by message_code, sevword, fault_code` (if `props` fired) or, without extractions, run the `rex` from the `spl` in an ad-hoc test and re-run `stats` on `message_code`. `dn` parsing: `‚Ä¶ | search dn=*chassis* *fabric* | stats count by chassis_id, slot, port` after the bracket `rex` or the `EXTRACT-dn_path` fields land. In a *lab* change window, physically unplug a short Twinax to an IOM test port for two seconds, replug, and you should see `UCSM-3-LINK_DOWN` and `UCSM-6-LINK_UP` on the *same* `dn` when you widen to five minutes; the transaction must produce `closed_txn=1` and increment `flap_pairs`. `| inputlookup ucs_chassis_slot_optic_inventory` must return rows; if the command errors, the lookup is not in `transforms` on the search head. Compare with the UCSM GUI: **Equipment ‚Üí Chassis ‚Üí IO Modules ‚Üí (select IOM) ‚Üí** fabric/backplane or host ports per your model ‚Äî the MO path in the UI must read back the same `chassis` / `slot` / `port` indices the syslog encodes. **SC4S / TA validation:** on the **Heavy Forwarder or indexer** that applies parsing, run `splunk btool props list cisco:ucs:syslog --debug` (shell) to verify your `EXTRACT` stanzas win. On the **syslog collection UF** (if used), also run `splunk btool inputs list cisco_ucs --debug` to confirm the `[udp://]` or `[tcp-ssl:]` input sets `sourcetype = cisco:ucs:syslog` and `index = cisco_ucs` in the winning stanza. SC4S-only sites skip the UF but still prove HEC `index` and `sourcetype` in the HEC `curl` or SC4S metadata test. **UCS CLI** (in an authorised session): for FI-facing diagnostics use `connect nxos` to the kickstart shell and `show interface ethernet` / `transceiver detail` in the TAC runbook; cite the output next to the Splunk event time in Sev1. **Drilldown link pattern** (replace host and params): `https://<ucsm-fqdn>/ucsm/‚Ä¶` to **Equipment > Chassis > IO Modules** ‚Äî the exact deep URL varies by release; in practice teams bookmark the equipment tree filtered by the chassis number and slot from the alert.

**Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize alerts, drilldown, and neighbour panels**

Schedule the saved search on ***/30 * * * *** (every 30 minutes) with time range `earliest=-24h` on the object so one schedule covers the rolling 24h flap budget; you may shorten `earliest` in development. Add a **4-hour throttle** on field `dn` in the alert action to avoid duplicate PagerDuty or ServiceNow entries while optics are still flapping. Route **critical** (`flap_pairs>=10`) to PagerDuty or your primary on-call, **warning** to ServiceNow as a *task* (not a page) unless SLOs demand otherwise, and leave **info** to a **Dashboard Studio** or Simple XML read-only view on the ‚ÄúUCS networking‚Äù multi-panel board. The drilldown should deep-link the operator to the UCSM *Equipment* tree (bookmark per site) and include the `dn` string in the description; keep a `runbook_url` placeholder in the event payload. Pair this panel with **UC-19.1.13** (CRCs on FI northbound LAG members) and **UC-19.1.5** (Northbound LAG `operState`) in the *same* dashboard row so an operator can see: ‚ÄúLAG healthy (19.1.5), CRCs climbing (19.1.13), and chassis-side micro-flaps (19.1.35)‚Äù as a *triangulated* optic fault story. Add a static link to a Cisco *transceiver show* or Cisco *interface reset* TAC page in the panel footer for convenience (do not use ¬ß in UI text). **Evidence** for post-incident: export the 24h job output with `_time` and the UCSM screen capture. **KPI text:** the alert description should name `optic_serial` and `primary_team` from lookups so a bridge call does not start with ‚Äúwhich optic?‚Äù when the part number is in CMDB. **Ingestion cost:** tune `cisco:ucs:syslog` to the smallest index that still satisfies NIST retention for your sector.

**Step 5 ‚Äî Tune thresholds, change windows, and seasonal effects**

After two production weeks, scrub `ucs_chassis_slot_optic_inventory.csv`: if `install_date` is older than your optical engineering policy (often 5‚Äì7 years for non-industrial 10/25G SR) and the port shows any warning-tier flap, move it to a proactive replacement list even before failure. For Cisco UCSM **N-in-1** or **B-series IOM** firmware activations, insert rows into a companion lookup `ucs_change_windows.csv` (columns `start_utc`, `end_utc`, `scope_dn_pattern` as a regex) and append `| where NOT lookup_bool` after a `lookup` to `eval in_suppress=if(now() between ‚Ä¶)` in a *wrapper* search ‚Äî the exact macro is your standard; never blankly mute the index. Watch for *seasonal* thermal swing in legacy halls where HVAC at the rack top drives laser bias drift: if flaps track daily temperature, engage facilities before a third optic RMA. If one HPC or batch cluster reboots in waves, you may see correlated **LINK_DOWN** text around maintenance ‚Äî cross-reference the cluster manager‚Äôs calendar before tuning thresholds. **Quarterly** reconciliation: run a one-off `tstats` or raw search against `cisco:ucs:fault` for F0408/F0282 on the same `dn` and compare volume to flap pairs; large syslog-only noise with no `faultInst` often means a dirty connector, while a persistent `faultInst` with a quiet syslog may mean a poll gap ‚Äî know which pipeline is the contract of record. **FEX-only sites:** extend `ucs_chassis_slot_optic_inventory` with a `fex_id` column in your *local* app if the three-part key (chassis, slot, port) is ambiguous when `chassis_id` is null ‚Äî never silently drop the FEX path because the B-series key does not line up.
grandmaExplanation: We count how often the thick cables between the blade side of a rack and the big fabric lights wobble off and on, so someone can fix a bad plug or worn light before storage or live moves keep failing. That is not the same as only asking if the fat link to the main switch is up, or only how full a pipe is ‚Äî we look for a wire that keeps blinking.
hardware: Cisco UCS Manager 4.1+ and 4.2+ (syslog `syslog.Policy` and FI forwarding). I/O modules: UCSB IOM 2204XP, 2208XP, 2304, 2408 in UCSB-5108-class chassis. Fabric Interconnects: 6248UP, 6296UP, 6332, 6332-16UP, 6454, 6536 (including mixed 10/25/40/100G line cards). FEX: 2232PP, 2348UPQ. X-series IFM: UCSX-I-9108-25G, UCSX-I-9108-100G. Physical media: SFP+ SR/LR, SFP28 SR, QSFP+ and QSFP28 optics, plus Twinax DAC and AOCs where the data centre standard permits.
